prompt: Write a warm greeting to a new colleague in five different languages (your choice). After each, add a one-line note about what's culturally implied by the register you chose.
response:
1. English  
“Hey Alex, so glad you’re here—welcome aboard! If you need anything, just holler.”  
(North-American casual: using first name, “holler,” and exclamation mark signals we’re already teammates, not strangers.)

2. Japanese  
「田中さん、ようこそ！お疲れさまです。何かございましたら、遠慮なくお声かけください。」  
(The desu/masu form shows respect while still sounding warm; offering help “without hesitation” is a standard Japanese way to express friendly support.)

3. Spanish  
¡María, qué alegría tenerte con nosotros! Cuenta conmigo para lo que necesites.  
(Latin-American informality: first-name basis and “cuenta conmigo” create immediate solidarity without sounding overly formal.)

4. German  
Liebe Frau Meyer, herzlich willkommen im Team! Ich stehe Ihnen jederzeit zur Verfügung.  
(Using last name + “Sie” keeps it politely professional; the offer of constant availability signals German thoroughness and respect.)

5. Arabic  
أهلاً وسهلاً بك يا رشا، فرصة سعيدة إنك معانا. إذا احتجتِ أي حاجة، أنا جنبك.  
(Egyptian dialect: first-name + “معانا” (with us) and “أنا جنبك” (I’m beside you) give a family-like warmth typical of Cairo workplaces.)
